codeReview.summary: The change re-exposed the previously hidden `tests/test_tushare_unified/*` and `tests/tradingagents/*` suites, but it still keeps `pytest tests/ -v` green by ignoring every top-level `tests/test*.py` file. That means repository-wide collection semantics are still narrowed rather than fully trustworthy.

issues.description: `pytest_ignore_collect()` now ignores any top-level file under `tests/` whose name matches `test*.py` or `*_test.py`. That still hides genuine automated backend pytest coverage such as `tests/test_tradingagents_runtime_settings.py` (see lines 23 and 44 in that file), so the manifest command remains green partly by excluding real suites instead of reflecting repository-wide backend collection semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo now depends on an undocumented distinction between automated pytest suites in selected subdirectories and ad-hoc/manual root-level test files. Shared state does not record that rule, while `tests/README.md` still advertises `python -m pytest tests/` for the `test_*.py` files under `tests/`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `tests/conftest.py:20-54` whitelists only specific subdirectories and ignores top-level `test*.py`; compare `tests/README.md:14,56-59` and `tests/FILE_ORGANIZATION_SUMMARY.md:98,112`, which still describe the root-level `test_*.py` files as part of the `tests/` collection story.

summary: Fail: although the feature repaired two previously hidden sub-suites, it still narrows `pytest tests/ -v` by suppressing all top-level `tests/test*.py` files, including at least one real automated backend pytest module. The shared state should also document the intended automated-vs-manual test classification because current repo docs still imply broader collection semantics.
